Title: Problem faced while giving the directional input
Problem: The issue where a controller (particularly a Bluetooth one) experiences problems, often losing directional input or fully disconnecting, when the phone screen turns off or goes to sleep while using Samsung DeX mode. This issue has been widely reported by users, especially after certain One UI software up (like One UI 8.0 on some devices). The most frequent reports indicate that when the phone's display sleeps, the controller's analog sticks and D-pad stop working, while buttons (A, B, X, Y, shoulder buttons) may continue to function intermittently or be unresponsive. The community consensus is that this is a software bug introduced in a recent One UI up specifically affecting how DeX manages Bluetooth devices when the host phone's display is powered down. It did not affect older versions of DeX.

Title: All lock screen widgets disappear when device is plugged to charging
Problem: I am using a Samsung Galaxy S24 FE, and I’ve recently noticed an intermittent issue while charging. Whenever I plug in my phone to charge, the screen automatically turns on, and the lock screen behaves abnormally — the icons and widgets disappear, leaving the screen mostly blank.
